clinical_coherence.notes: 68F African American with AF (self-discontinued warfarin 4 months ago), HTN, DM2, HFpEF, OSA, CKD3a, obesity. Presenting with sudden right hemiplegia and expressive aphasia. Vitals appropriate: BP 192/104, HR 108 (AF with RVR). Left-handed patient with left-hemisphere language dominance confirmed by aphasia pattern. Neuro exam consistent with left MCA superior division territory stroke.

tool_output_consistency.notes: All tools concordant. MRI shows left MCA superior division DWI restriction with M1/M2 occlusion. ECG confirms AF with RVR at 108. Labs show INR 1.1 confirming non-therapeutic anticoagulation, CKD at baseline, hyperglycemia. Echo shows preserved EF with dilated LA. All consistent with cardioembolic mechanism.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD I63.9 used again (non-specific). Critical actions are comprehensive including CKD-adjusted DOAC dosing consideration. Contraindicated actions appropriately include administering contrast without premedication for documented allergy. Key reasoning about left-handedness and language dominance is clinically sophisticated.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Labeled straightforward, which is appropriate for the core diagnosis. The case has slightly more complexity than S01 (left-handedness, contrast allergy, CKD dosing), but the diagnostic path remains clear. Could arguably be moderate given these management complexities, but the diagnosis itself is straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: Highly realistic. 68F African American with the well-documented higher stroke risk in this population. Multiple comorbidities (AF, HTN, DM2, HFpEF, OSA, CKD, obesity) form a believable metabolic syndrome cluster. Self-discontinuation of warfarin due to bruising is a common real-world scenario.

educational_value.notes: Teaches about health disparities in stroke, left-handedness and language dominance, CKD-adjusted anticoagulation, contrast allergy management in emergent CTA, and the common problem of warfarin non-adherence. Rich educational content beyond the basic stroke algorithm.

strengths: Addresses health disparities and real-world adherence challenges
Sophisticated handling of left-handedness and language dominance
Multiple management considerations beyond basic stroke protocol
